revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:糸井大塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Itoi Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Itoi Otsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
Full view of the mound
(Central circular mound, rectangular front visible to the right) | alternate_name = Tsuka-no-moto Tomb No. 1 | part_of = Tsuka-no-moto Kofun Group | location = {{ill|Miyoshi, Hiroshima|en|Miyoshi, Hiroshima|qid=Q840591|lt=Miyoshi City|1=Miyoshi, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Itoi Town | coordinates = {{Coord|34.748611|N|132.899014|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| omitted = JP-34 | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total mound length: 65 m
Height: 8-10 m | burial = Unknown | burial goods = Fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | Satellite Tombs = | built = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} ([[Kofun period]]) | Interred_individual = | Mausoleum = | Historical Status = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historic Site | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | type = Scallop-shaped Kofun-SE | map_dot_label = Itoi Ōtsuka
Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883|1=Scallop-shaped kofun}} }} '''Itoi Ōtsuka Kofun''' (糸井大塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Miyoshi, Hiroshima|en|Miyoshi, Hiroshima|qid=Q840591|lt=Miyoshi City|1=Miyoshi, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Itoi Town. It is a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and has been Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == Situated on the southern edge of the [[Miyoshi Basin]], this large scallop-shaped Kofun was constructed on the river terrace of the Minabara River{{Sfn|塚の本古墳（平凡社）|1982}}. No archaeological excavations have been conducted to date. The mound has a distinctive scallop type, with a short rectangular section extending southeast from a circular section. Measuring 65 meters in total mound length, it is the largest scallop-shaped kofun in Hiroshima Prefecture. The mound surface reveals fragments of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, with house-shaped haniwa found near the rectangular section. A surrounding moat, approximately 30 meters wide, encircles the mound, although its width narrows on the western side due to the typeer presence of Tomb No. 2 (now lost). Details of the burial facility remain unknown. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} during the middleKofun period. Hiroshima Prefecture contains numerous scallop-shaped kofun, with a notable concentration in the Miyoshi region. These kofun provide valuable insights into the relationship between the region and the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}}Exhibition catalog: ''The Mystery of Kofun in the Mist - The built of the Great Kings and Miyoshi Basin''. Hiroshima Prefectural History and Folklore Museum, 2018, pp. 19-31.. The site was Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site in {{ill|1994|en|1994|qid=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}} (Heisei 6). Surrounding the main mound, numerous other kofun have been identified. == Mound == [[File:Itoi Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mound summit}}]] [[File:Itoi Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|View of the rectangular section and moat from the mound summit}}]] The area of the mound are as followsSite description board (Miyoshi City Board of Education, installed in 2010).: * Total mound length: Over 100 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Mound length: 65 meters * Circular section: ''' Diameter: 56 meters ''' Height: 8-10 meters * Rectangular section: ''' Width: 19-20 meters ''' Height: Estimated 3 meters The surrounding moat's outline can still be observed in the nearby rice paddies. == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: ''' Itoi Ōtsuka Kofun (Itoi Tsuka-no-moto Tomb No. 1) - Designated on October 31, 1994[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121220.html Itoi Ōtsuka Kofun (Itoi Tsuka-no-moto Tomb No. 1)] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Description board (Miyoshi City Board of Education, 2010 installation) * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 35: Hiroshima Prefecture|editor=|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|year=1982|isbn=4582490352|chapter=Tsuka-no-moto Kofun|ref={{Harvid|塚の本古墳（平凡社）|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|editor=|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|chapter=Ōtsuka Kofun > Itoi Ōtsuka Kofun|ref={{Harvid|糸井大塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == Related Works == {{small|(Works not directly used in this article)}} * {{Cite book|title=Tsuka-no-moto Kofun Group|editor=|publisher=Miyoshi City|year=2004|ref=}} == External Links == * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206121220.html Itoi Ōtsuka Kofun (Itoi Tsuka-no-moto Tomb No. 1)] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education "Hotline Education Hiroshima" {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|糸井大塚古墳|version=93895276|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Itoi Otsuka Kofun}} [[ja:糸井大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q61750529}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Miyoshi]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
